concepts.name: Disjoint Sets
concepts.short_definition: A collection of sets where no two sets share common elements.

concepts.name: Union Operation
concepts.short_definition: Merges two disjoint sets into a single set.

concepts.name: Find Operation
concepts.short_definition: Determines which set a particular element belongs to.

concepts.name: Path Compression
concepts.short_definition: An optimization for the Find operation that flattens the tree structure.

concepts.name: Union by Rank
concepts.short_definition: An optimization for the Union operation that attaches the shorter tree to the root of the taller tree.

concepts.name: Union by Size
concepts.short_definition: An optimization for the Union operation that attaches the smaller tree to the root of the larger tree.

concepts.name: Forest Representation
concepts.short_definition: Representing disjoint sets as a collection of trees, where each tree represents a set.

concepts.name: Parent Array
concepts.short_definition: An array used to store the parent of each element in the forest representation.

concepts.name: Rank/Size Array
concepts.short_definition: An array used to store the rank or size of each tree in the forest representation for optimization.

concepts.name: Applications
concepts.short_definition: Real-world problems that can be solved efficiently using the Disjoint Set Union data structure.
